title: Chewy Q2 Preview: Are Pet Stocks Defensive? Results May Show 'Stable Industry Trends'
text: Chewy's Q2 2025 earnings preview suggested the company would demonstrate the defensive characteristics of the pet industry. The broader pet market has been characterized as stable and recession-resistant, with consumers prioritizing pet care even during economic downturns. Pre-earnings analysis indicated consensus expectations for modest sequential growth with continued margin expansion. The preview highlighted that non-discretionary spending on pet supplies—approximately 85% of Chewy's revenue—provides downside protection during recessions. Analyst sentiment leaned toward viewing Chewy as a defensive play with resilient demand dynamics. The strong autoship-driven revenue model was highlighted as a key factor in demand predictability. Expectations focused on whether Chewy would maintain its market share gains in a normalizing pet industry and whether margin expansion could accelerate further through operational leverage and high-margin initiatives.
